refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis without implying any implementation or combining multiple functions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an accurate analysis of the worksite terrain independently of other site features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and policy overlays
Provide an interface for planning and execution modules to query terrain information
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a foundational terrain model that can be independently developed and then used by other functions, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate raw terrain data for model generation
Risk that terrain model may not capture all features required by safety and operational policies if not properly specified
Assumes planning and execution functions can integrate with the terrain model interface
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for integrating safety and operational policy overlays to ensure compliance and completeness in the terrain analysis.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses data processing and environmental modeling functions, including digital terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data and site feature inputs necessary for terrain modeling.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to initiate terrain model generation tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models to other system components or external users.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion as outputs are shared via network link.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on mobility and control; terrain modeling would require extensive data sharing and cause diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; terrain modeling is outside its scope, causing high diffusion.
Safety Services are unrelated to terrain modeling, leading to very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP, as it naturally contains environmental data processing functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
Interface changes in Telemetry or Network Link could impact module integration.
If terrain modeling algorithms require real-time updates, latency in data transfer could pose challenges.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by producing a digital representation of the terrain, which is a clear and measurable functional requirement.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high cohesion for terrain modeling functions and minimal diffusion risk. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the availability of the generated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions, as specified in acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly aligns with the DP and FR, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry input, tasking commands, and network output, ensuring clear allocation and integration.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on telemetry data quality and timeliness to ensure model accuracy.
Establish robust interface change management processes for Telemetry and Network Link to maintain integration stability.
Assess and mitigate potential latency issues if real-time terrain model updates are required.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and no diffusion risks. The DP fully satisfies the FR, the DP is well contained within a high-cohesion implementation module, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Recommended revisions are for monitoring and managing dependencies rather than structural changes. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here and focus on implementing and verifying the current design before further decomposition.

revise.why: No structural revisions needed at this time; only monitoring and management recommendations.

assumptions: Telemetry data quality and timeliness can be monitored and managed effectively.
Interfaces for telemetry input, tasking commands, and network output remain stable with change management processes.
open_risks: Potential latency issues if real-time terrain model updates are required.
Dependency on telemetry data quality impacting model accuracy.
evidence_needed: Verification results of the digital terrain model accuracy and timeliness.
Interface stability and integration test results between NGHE Solution and telemetry/network modules.
